awards: Princeton University Center for Human Values Thesis Prize
Princeton University Department of Anthropology Thesis Prize
Streicker International Fellowship for Comumunity Engagement, Rome 2017

sections.content: Visual and poetic ethnography. Interdisciplinary research for a more just and equitable world.

body.title: Ethnographic Fieldwork
body.content: Fieldwork carried out in Rome, and Catania, Sicily. I carried out 10-weeks of ethnographic research for my Anthropology thesis on the urban experience of Eritrean refugees living in Rome. I focused on how different city spaces (religious, squatted buildings and migrant camps) inhabited by Eritreans become sites of resistance to both the Italian and Eritrean state.

body.title: San’Egidio: Integration Officer
body.content: I worked in the Community’s integration program for refugees in their Italian and English school. I also travelled to Jerusalem, Israel for two-weeks to assist on the Community’s peace and conflict resolution project with Palestinian Christians. In September, I helped organize the Paths of Peace Meeting in Münster, Germany- an international summit of religious and state-leaders on the role of religion in global migration.

body.title: JRN 465: Reporting on the Frontlines of History in Greece
body.content: Student Journalist Athens and Lesbos Island, Greece, Princeton University Center for Journalism. Wrote a long-form story on the refugee situation in Greece. The team reported on breaking news stories and videos published in eKathermini, Greek Partner to the New York Times.

body.title: The Clay Project: Co-Founder and Co-President
body.content: An advocacy-art project to promote awareness on migration issues through a collaboration with the Wilson Ceramics Studio at Princeton University and the Amal Foundation in Amman, Jordan. Partnered with Princeton town businesses to sell hand-made ceramics. Profits from sales fully-funded scholarships for two Syrian refugees living in Zaatari Refugee Camp to attend university in Jordan.

body.title: Princeton University
body.content: Class of 2019. Summa Cum Laude [with highest honors] AB in Anthropology. Certificate in Values and Public Life. Coursework includes: Colonialism and Historical Injustice; Human Rights; Political Anthropology; Policing and Militarization; Global Forced Migration Studies; The Anthropology of Development.
